name: Browserless
tagline: Deploy headless browsers in Docker – manage them locally or run on cloud
description: Browserless provides infrastructure and APIs to run and scale headless browser sessions using Chromium-based and other browsers, enabling automation, testing, scraping, PDF/screenshot rendering, and more. It comes as an open-source Docker image with optional managed cloud services.

features.title: Headless Browser Automation
features.description: Run and control headless browser instances (Chrome/Chromium/WebKit/Firefox) using standard libraries such as Puppeteer and Playwright.

features.title: Parallel & Scalable Sessions
features.description: Supports multiple concurrent browser sessions with queueing and configurable concurrency limits.

features.title: REST APIs for Browser Tasks
features.description: Built-in APIs for common tasks such as generating PDFs, taking screenshots, and retrieving HTML.

features.title: Session Management and Debugging
features.description: Manage browser sessions with health checks, timeouts, reconnects, and visual debug viewers.

features.title: Containerized Deployment
features.description: Runs as a Docker image with all required system fonts and dependencies pre-included.

features.title: Automation Workflows & Integrations
features.description: Integrates with workflows and tools like n8n and script code via WebSockets or REST.

features.title: Screenshot & PDF Generation
features.description: Programmatically capture web page screenshots and generate PDFs.

features.title: Support for Multiple Browsers
features.description: Includes out-of-the-box support for Chromium, WebKit, Firefox, and more via separate images.

features.title: Bot Detection Mitigation
features.description: Optional advanced features to handle bot detection and CAPTCHAs (in premium plans).
